business.customerType: German SMEs in manufacturing, automotive, and engineering; HR / innovation leads; CTOs
business.bestSeller: 90-minute AI Agents workshop for non-technical teams
differentiators: We ship working agents in production, not slide decks
German-speaking, EU-based, no language friction with mid-market buyers
Hands-on - every workshop has a live demo, every proposal has a working prototype
Founder-led delivery; the person you talk to is the person who builds it

segments.name: Primary - Innovation managers at German SMEs
segments.demo: 35-55, manufacturing / automotive / engineering, 50-500 employees, English business-fluent but German-native
segments.psycho: Has heard 'AI' in every board meeting for 2 years. Burned by big-consultancy demos that never shipped. Looking for someone who has actually built and deployed something.
segments.where: LinkedIn (90% of work-time browsing), industry conferences, founder podcasts

segments.name: Secondary - CTOs and dev leads
segments.demo: 30-45, technical, evaluating AI tooling for internal use
segments.psycho: Wants concrete tool comparisons (N8N vs Claude Code vs OpenClaw), production deployment patterns, no marketing fluff.

segments.name: Tertiary - HR and people-ops
segments.demo: 35-55, HR / L&D, considering AI literacy training for their team
segments.psycho: Needs a credible partner for company-wide AI workshops. Risk-averse on vendor choice. Wants references.

painPoints: Built a chatbot pilot, never reached production
Team can't tell N8N from Make from Zapier from Claude Code
Board pushing for an 'AI strategy' but no in-house expertise
Big consultancies quoted six figures for what should be a 4-week project
desires: One working AI agent shipped in 30 days
Team that understands the tools well enough to maintain
A credible AI story to tell their own customers
Stop seeing AI as magic; start treating it as engineering
competitors: Internal IT teams trying to DIY

do: Lead with what shipped, not what's possible
Show the workflow on screen - real screenshots, not abstract diagrams
Cite the tool by name (Claude Code, N8N, OpenClaw, Postiz)
Speak as the founder, in first person
Number your lists when there's a sequence
dont: Use stock photos of robots or glowing brains
Hide pricing or scope (we lead with a fixed 30-day frame)
Apologize for being a small team - that's the offer
Use AI-generated stock-look images for thought-leadership posts (always real screenshots or photos)

viktorCanDoWithoutAsking: Draft LinkedIn posts in Martin's voice (run them by him before any post)
Generate diagram concepts via Nano Banana (never AI-stock-photos)
Propose post ideas grounded in shipped work
viktorNeedsApprovalFor: Any post that mentions a client by name
Any post that quotes a specific revenue number
Any post that references unreleased / unshipped work
Any reply to a DM (Martin handles inbound himself)
Any post under 'we are' positioning - founder voice only
sensitiveTopics: Client confidentiality - never name a project's client unless explicitly OK'd
Pricing - never publish a fee structure
Crypto / web3 endorsements (we don't do this)

metricsThatMatter: Inbound LinkedIn DMs from buyer titles (CTO, head of innovation, HR director at SMEs)
Post saves on tactical / technical content (signal that the right audience is using it)

examples: Anthropic's product page copy - confident but specific
Latent Space podcast notes - engineer-grade detail
Pieter Levels's solo-founder updates - real screenshots, real numbers

expectations: Within 90 days, GF's LinkedIn should produce at least 2 qualified inbound conversations per week from buyer titles at German SMEs, with zero manual posting effort from Martin. Workshops should fill via inbound, not outbound.
